title: Responsible use of the CVR functions
text: Four functions, one step : build query, send , extract hits, download documents. Requires curl jsonlite. cvr_query() builds Elasticsearch query (pure, network). Matches CVR number (max 1000 per query) accounting-period end date. cvr_search() posts query returns parsed response. cvr_hits() flattens response tibble one row per document (cvrnummer, dokumenturl, …). cvr_download() downloads documents, named CVR number. Everything cvr_search() local; line first real API call.
code: cvr_query(   cvr          = c("12345678", "87654321"),   enddate_from = "2024-01-01",   enddate_to   = "2024-12-31" ) |>   cvr_search(contact = "you@example.com") |>   cvr_hits() |>   cvr_download("data/pdf")

title: Getting started with daos
text: Reading set files normally takes small pipeline: list.files(), loop purrr::map(), manual naming. read_files() one call handles path expansion, existence checks, format detection, naming along way. single path returns object directly; multiple paths return named list progress bar. Supported formats: csv, tsv, parquet, feather, xlsx, xls, rds, sas7bdat, sav, por, xpt, dta, json, ndjson, jsonl, yaml, yml, txt. practice, read_files() works best data production consistent: formats, column names, types across files. read_files() handles set files without warnings, workflow order. warns type mismatches, something changed upstream. separate article way working, using view_types() files refuse stack: vignette("read-files"). Supply custom reader override auto-detection add arguments: Column name casing default, column names converted lowercase reading (.lowercase = TRUE). Set .lowercase = FALSE preserve original casing. Binding one tibble Set = "bind" row-bind files. Use .id track origin row. names numeric vector (e.g. years), .id column also numeric. column types differ across files, warning issued types reconciled readr::type_convert(). .id column always excluded reconciliation. Unpacking individual variables Set = "unpack" assign file named variable calling environment. default, read_files() aborts name already exists. Set .overwrite = TRUE allow overwriting.
code: # Single file (returns object directly): df <- read_files("data/results.parquet")  # Multiple files with glue expansion: lst <- read_files("data/dat{0:9}.parquet", names = 0:9) read_files(   "data/dat{0:9}.parquet",   reader = \(x) arrow::read_parquet(x, col_select = 1:5) ) read_files(   "data/dat{2020:2024}.parquet",   names = 2020:2024,   out   = "bind",   .id   = "year" ) read_files("data/dat{0:9}.parquet", names = paste0("dat", 0:9), out = "unpack")

what: write_excel() / append_excel(): write presentable Excel files
title: Getting started with daos
text: two functions exist plain data export hard read people file meant . complaint colleague works macroeconomic statistics concrete: frozen header, thousand separators, rounding. writexl::write_xlsx() fast writes exactly unformatted files, openxlsx2 can styling wants workbook object, worksheets, styles, save call. write_excel() sits : one call, defaults make file presentable. Numeric columns least one value ≥ 1,000 formatted thousand separator displayed decimals (#,##0); underlying values preserved. Year-like columns excluded automatically: numeric column every value whole number 1800 2200 assumed hold years, 2020 displayed 2.020. Disable detect_years = FALSE, use skip_fmt columns heuristic guess (e.g. numeric period codes like 202001). NA values appear blank cells. header row bold. first row frozen (can turned freeze_header = FALSE). .xlsx can written. old binary .xls format supported, non-.xlsx path fails early instead producing file Excel complain . Writing new file Pass data frame named list data frames. Requires overwrite = TRUE file already exists. Sheet naming Sheet names come list names. Unnamed elements get default names ("Sheet1", "Sheet2", …). Mixed naming also fine: Appending sheets existing file append_excel() adds sheets without touching existing content. file must already exist, overwrite = TRUE required sheet name already present. options
code: # Single data frame: sheet name defaults to "Sheet1" write_excel(mtcars, "output.xlsx")  # Named list: each element becomes a sheet write_excel(   list(Cars = mtcars, Iris = iris),   "output.xlsx" ) # "Hoved" is explicit; second sheet becomes "Sheet2" write_excel(list(Hoved = mtcars, iris), "output.xlsx") write_excel(list(Hoved = mtcars), "output.xlsx") append_excel(list(Bilag = iris), "output.xlsx") # Insert as an Excel table (filter arrows, banded rows) write_excel(mtcars, "output.xlsx", as_table = TRUE)  # Exclude columns from the #,##0 format write_excel(mtcars, "output.xlsx", skip_fmt = "hp")

what: view_types(): compare column types across data frames

what: expect_empty(): assert a data frame is empty
title: Getting started with daos
text: Often want assert filter finds nothing, hear right away . Writing custom (nrow(x) > 0) cli::cli_abort(...) every time gets repetitive. expect_empty() one step, fits pipeline, can write log file. Filter rows exist, pipe , offending rows hand moment something goes wrong. log argument writes timestamped entry file, scheduled run misbehaves, errors already collected time sit investigate:
code: # Success: dplyr::starwars |>   filter(name == "Harry Potter") |>   expect_empty(     success_msg = "No Harry Potter rows in starwars",     warn_msg    = "Unexpected fictional character found"   ) #> ✔ No Harry Potter rows in starwars # Warning: rows found dplyr::starwars |>   filter(mass > 1000) |>   expect_empty(warn_msg = "Unrealistic mass values") #> Warning: Unrealistic mass values # Hard abort: dplyr::starwars |>   filter(mass > 1000) |>   expect_empty(abort_msg = "Unrealistic mass values") log_path <- f("log/{nowf()}/checks.log") checker  <- \(data, ...) expect_empty(data, ..., log = log_path)  dplyr::starwars |>   filter(name == "Harry Potter") |>   checker(success_msg = "No fictional characters")  dplyr::starwars |>   filter(height > 250) |>   checker(warn_msg = "Unusually tall characters")

what: Anything filter() can express is a rule
title: Lightweight data validation with checkpoints
text: strength pattern rule language just dplyr. checkpoints real pipelines:
code: # Identifiers must look right (%like% preserves NA, unlike grepl) df |>   filter(!cvr %like% "^\\d{8}$") |>   expect_empty(abort_msg = "Malformed CVR numbers")  # Amounts must reconcile within tolerance df |>   filter(abs(total - rowSums(across(starts_with("post_")))) > 1) |>   expect_empty(warn_msg = "Totals do not reconcile")  # Categorical values must be in the codebook df |>   filter(!branche %in% codebook$branche) |>   expect_empty(abort_msg = "Unknown industry codes")  # Every expected unit must be present (anti-join as violation set) expected |>   anti_join(df, by = "cvr") |>   expect_empty(warn_msg = "Units missing from the delivery")

text: Text file format: line either category line data line: Category line: single string field delimiter. Becomes note column subsequent data lines. Data line: three fields separated min_spaces consecutive spaces: (1) element name, (2) amount year, (3) amount year - 1. Amounts must whole kroner (periods thousands separators stripped automatically). previous year absent, third field may empty becomes NA. Appending  statnatio category line negates values category (useful costs appear positive sign notes). title: Add information derived from Danish CPR numbers — add_cpr_info
text: Supported info types (add values): valid means, : valid = TRUE requires exactly two things: cleaned value ten digits, digits encode real calendar date official century rules. modulus-11 check part valid. Since 2007 CPR office assigned numbers without modulus-11 control, birth dates run mod-11-compatible sequence numbers. cpr.dk states "fuldt ud gyldige personnumre" (far assigned persons born certain 1 January dates 1960 1990s). failed mod-11 therefore make CPR number invalid. Validators reject mod-11 wrongly reject real, living people. check still reported separately mod11, works data quality signal: high failure rate older data suggests keying errors. official assignment series start sequence number 0001, 0000 never occurs practice. rejected , since date check documented criterion. Century detection follows official CPR rules based digit 7 two-digit year component (see table source code). Input tolerance: CPR column standardised clean_cpr(): dashes spaces stripped nine-digit numbers zero-padded left (recovering values lost leading zero Excel). CPR column returned data frame always returned standardised 10-digit format xxxxxxxxxx. Implementation note: function plain vectorised arithmetic: one string--number conversion, digits peeled n--10 matrix, mod-11 checksum single matrix product, birth dates constructed directly epoch day counts (date-string parsing). scales linearly millions rows.

title: Add information derived from Danish CPR numbers — add_cpr_info
text: CPR-kontoret, Personnummeret CPR-systemet (1 July 2008), https://cdn2.gopublic.dk/cpr/media/12066/personnummeret--cpr.pdf; Opbygning af CPR-nummeret, https://www.cpr.dk/cpr-systemet/opbygning-af-cpr-nummeret; Personnumre uden kontrolciffer (modulus 11 kontrol), https://www.cpr.dk/cpr-systemet/personnumre-uden-kontrolciffer-modulus-11-kontrol.

what: Read one or more files — read_files
title: Read one or more files — read_files
text: Expands paths using glue::glue() ({0:9} generates ten paths), checks every file exists, reads automatic format detection custom reader, optionally row-binds unpacks result.

title: Read one or more files — read_files
code: read_files(   paths,   names = NULL,   reader = "auto",   out = NULL,   .envir = parent.frame(),   .id = NULL,   .overwrite = FALSE,   .lowercase = TRUE,   ... )

title: Read one or more files — read_files
text: paths character vector file paths. Glue syntax ({}) supported compact range expansion. names Optional names result. Defaults file names without extension. numeric, .id column (= "bind") also numeric. reader "auto" (default) detect format file extension, function \(path, ...) ... use custom reader. Controls returned reading: NULL (default): object directly single file; named list multiple files. "bind": row-bind data frames single tibble. column types differ, warning issued types reconciled readr::type_convert(). source column added .id set. "unpack": assign element named variable .envir. .envir Environment used glue interpolation (= "unpack") target assignment. Default calling frame. .id Name source column added = "bind". NULL (default), source column added. .overwrite FALSE (default), aborts name already exists .envir = "unpack". Set TRUE allow overwrites. .lowercase TRUE (default), column names converted lowercase reading. Set FALSE preserve original casing. ... Additional arguments forwarded reader function.

title: Read one or more files — read_files
text: Depends : NULL: object (single file) named list (multiple files). "bind": single tibble. "unpack": named list, invisibly.

title: Read one or more files — read_files
text: Supported formats (reader = "auto"): Note CSV: uses readr::read_csv2() expects semicolon-separated values comma decimal mark (Danish/European convention). comma-separated files, pass custom reader: reader = readr::read_csv. Note Excel: first sheet read. workbook several name one, warning lists others. Read specific sheet forwarding argument: read_files("x.xlsx", sheet = "Sheet2"). Packages non-base formats (arrow, haven, readxl, jsonlite, yaml) must installed separately.

title: Read one or more files — read_files
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single file (returns object directly): df <- read_files("data/results.parquet")  # Multiple files with glue expansion: lst <- read_files("data/dat{0:9}.parquet", names = 0:9)  # Custom reader: lst <- read_files(   "data/dat{0:9}.parquet",   reader = \(x) arrow::read_parquet(x, col_select = 1:5) )  # Read and bind into one tibble: df <- read_files("data/dat{0:9}.parquet", names = 0:9, out = "bind")  # Read and unpack into individual variables (dat0, dat1, ...): read_files("data/dat{0:9}.parquet", names = paste0("dat", 0:9), out = "unpack") summon("^dat\\d+$") } # }

title: Interactive time-series screening dashboard — screen_timeseries
text: Launches Shiny application visually reviewing time-series data group group. columns x, y, series, excluded become grouping dimensions dropdown selectors. Navigate groups arrow keys. Press Space flag current combination, R reset zoom, Q quit.

code: screen_timeseries(   data,   x,   y,   series = NULL,   .exclude = NULL,   .title = NULL,   .y_min = NULL,   .y_max = NULL )

title: Interactive time-series screening dashboard — screen_timeseries
text: data data frame containing minimum x-axis column, y-axis column, least one grouping column. x Time (x-axis) variable. Accepts Date, POSIXct, numeric years, value coercible date. (Unquoted.) y Numeric measurement (y-axis) variable. (Unquoted.) series Optional variable plotting multiple lines per group (e.g. category). (Unquoted.) .exclude Columns exclude becoming grouping dropdowns. (Unquoted, tidy-select.) .title Optional title string shown app header downloaded figures. group combination (· b · c) always shown separately affected. .y_min Optional numeric. Pre-fills Y min input, fixing lower bound y-axis globally across groups. Leave NULL automatic scaling. Set 0 replicate old "start zero" behaviour. .y_max Optional numeric. Pre-fills Y max input, fixing upper bound y-axis globally across groups. Leave NULL automatic scaling.

what: Interactive explorer for the Greenland Statbank — statbank_app
title: Interactive explorer for the Greenland Statbank — statbank_app
text: Launches Shiny app working Greenland Statbank (bank.stat.gl). app guides user three steps: find table (search titles, walk subject tree three-column browser – parent, current, live preview item cursor – also responds h/j/k/l arrow keys), choose values variable, fetch data. result shown table plot, app always shows statbank_get() call reproduces selection, click-built query can pasted straight script.

title: Interactive explorer for the Greenland Statbank — statbank_app
code: statbank_app(bank = "gl", lang = NULL)

title: Interactive explorer for the Greenland Statbank — statbank_app
text: bank Statbank open: "gl" (Greenland, default) "fo" (Faroe Islands). bank can also switched inside app. lang Language titles labels, NULL (default) bank's default (Danish Greenland, Faroese Faroe Islands).

title: Interactive explorer for the Greenland Statbank — statbank_app
text: last fetched dataset, invisibly (NULL nothing fetched).

title: Interactive explorer for the Greenland Statbank — statbank_app
text: Time variables, numeric variables many values (age), selected /dropdowns. variables open popup searchable checkbox list, select/deselect-shortcuts, running count; empty selection means values. settings panel step 3 toggles statbank_get() options: codes column names, codes cells, automatic type conversion. Fetched data can downloaded formatted Excel file (via write_excel() openxlsx2 installed, otherwise CSV), R code can copied one click. Pressing Q closes app returns last fetched dataset, df <- statbank_app() also works data-fetching workflow. app covers Greenland statbank (default) Faroese one. bank chooser sits one level subject root: press h (click breadcrumb) root switch bank, reloads tree bank's default language. table list fetched app starts (one request per folder tree) reused rest R session.

title: Interactive explorer for the Greenland Statbank — statbank_app
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ statbank_app() } # }

what: Download a table from a statbank — statbank_get
title: Download a table from a statbank — statbank_get
text: Fetches data table returns tibble long format: one column per variable value column numbers.

title: Download a table from a statbank — statbank_get
code: statbank_get(   table,   ...,   lang = NULL,   bank = "gl",   .col_names = c("text", "code"),   .values = c("text", "code"),   .type_convert = TRUE )

title: Download a table from a statbank — statbank_get
text: table table's path tree, e.g. "/BE01/BEXSAT1.PX". bare table id (e.g. "BEXSAT1.PX") also works table list fetched statbank_tables() statbank_search() session. ... Named selections, one per variable. Values can value codes, value texts, "*" . option arguments dot-prefixed, variable can never shadowed one . lang Language titles labels, NULL (default) bank's default. Greenland offers "da", "kl", "en"; Faroe Islands offer "fo", "en". bank statbank: "gl" (Greenland, default) "fo" (Faroe Islands). .col_names "text" (default) names columns variables' display texts; "code" uses variable codes. .values "text" (default) fills cells value display texts; "code" uses value codes. .type_convert TRUE (default), result passed readr::type_convert(), e.g. year column comes back numeric. Set FALSE keep every variable column character.

title: Download a table from a statbank — statbank_get
text: tibble one column per variable value column. table's footnotes provenance ride along attributes: attr(df, "notes"), attr(df, "source"), attr(df, "updated"), attr(df, "contact").

title: Download a table from a statbank — statbank_get
text: Selections passed named arguments. Names matched variable codes display texts, values value codes value texts, statbank_get("/BE01/BEXSAT1.PX", tid = 2024, art = "Antal") works without knowing internal codes. Matching ignores case folds Danish letters (foedested matches f\u00f8dested). Use "*" select values; variables mentioned default values. default, columns named variables' display texts chosen language, cells hold display texts values. Set .col_names = "code" /.values = "code" get internal codes instead, e.g. coded column (sex 0/1) wanted joins modelling. option arguments dot-prefixed can never collide variable name ....

title: Download a table from a statbank — statbank_get
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- statbank_get(   "BE/BE01/BEXSAT1.PX",   tid = c(2023, 2024, 2025),   art = "Antal" ) attr(df, "notes")  # Codes instead of display texts, no type conversion: df <- statbank_get(   "BE/BE01/BEXSAT1.PX",   tid = 2024,   .col_names = "code",   .values = "code",   .type_convert = FALSE ) } # }

what: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
title: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
text: Searches environment objects whose names match regular expression returns named list. Useful collecting family similarly-named objects (e.g. dat0 dat9) unpacking list = "unpack" read_files().

title: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
code: summon(pattern, .envir = parent.frame())

title: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
text: pattern single regular expression string. .envir environment search. Default calling environment.

title: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
text: named list matching objects.

title: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
code: dat1 <- data.frame(x = 1) dat2 <- data.frame(x = 2) dat3 <- data.frame(x = 3) summon("^dat\\d+$") #> $dat1 #>   x #> 1 1 #>  #> $dat2 #>   x #> 1 2 #>  #> $dat3 #>   x #> 1 3 #>

title: Add a task — task_add
text: Add task

title: Add a task — task_add
code: task_add(   db,   description,   key = NULL,   project = NULL,   assignee = NULL,   tags = NULL,   priority = NULL,   due = NULL,   recur = NULL,   depends = NULL )

title: Add a task — task_add
text: db Path task database, open DBI connection. description task text (required). key Optional user-chosen handle reference task – slug lowercase letters/digits joined single - _ (e.g. "compile-accounts"). Must unique across database, accepted anywhere id . Lets production script refer task stable, readable name instead brittle integer. numeric id uuid keep working regardless. project Optional project name. assignee Optional person task assigned . tags Optional character vector tags. priority Optional priority: "H", "M", "L". due Optional due date (Date "YYYY-MM-DD"). recur Optional recurrence applied task completed: integer number days, one "daily", "weekly", "biweekly", "monthly", "quarterly", "yearly". Needs due. depends Optional ids (integer, uuid, key) task depends .

title: Add a task — task_add
text: new task one-row tibble, invisibly.

title: Add a task — task_add
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ task_add("tasks.sqlite", "Write the report", project = "Q3",          tags = c("writing", "urgent"), priority = "H", due = "2026-07-01")  # Give it a key, then refer to it by that key later: task_add("tasks.sqlite", "Compile accounts", key = "compile-accounts") task_done("tasks.sqlite", "compile-accounts") } # }

title: List tasks — task_list

title: List tasks — task_list
text: db Path task database, open DBI connection. status Status filter: "pending" (default), "completed", "deleted", "active" (pending plus completed, .e. everything soft-deleted), "". project Optional project filter. assignee Optional person filter. tag Optional tag filter (kept task carries tag). sort One "urgency" (default), "due" (forfaldsdato), "entry" (oprettelsesdato), "project". desc TRUE, reverse sort order (e.g. latest due date newest task first). Tasks due date project stay last either way. .only_uuid Internal: restrict single uuid.

title: Modify a task — task_modify

title: Modify a task — task_modify

title: Modify a task — task_modify
text: db Path task database, open DBI connection. id Task identifier: integer id, uuid, key. description task text (required). key Optional user-chosen handle reference task – slug lowercase letters/digits joined single - _ (e.g. "compile-accounts"). Must unique across database, accepted anywhere id . Lets production script refer task stable, readable name instead brittle integer. numeric id uuid keep working regardless. project Optional project name. assignee Optional person task assigned . tags Optional character vector tags. priority Optional priority: "H", "M", "L". due Optional due date (Date "YYYY-MM-DD"). recur Optional recurrence applied task completed: integer number days, one "daily", "weekly", "biweekly", "monthly", "quarterly", "yearly". Needs due. depends Optional ids (integer, uuid, key) task depends .

title: Modify a task — task_modify
text: TRUE, invisibly.

title: Modify a task — task_modify
text: Pass key set rename task's key; pass key = "" remove . new key must still unique. Pass tags depends replace task's tags dependencies (use character(0) clear ); pass recur = "" stop task recurring.

what: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
title: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
text: Shows column types one data frames side--side. Useful diagnosing type mismatches join binding read_files().

title: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
code: view_types(..., diff = FALSE, focus = NULL)

title: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
text: ... Data frames compare (unquoted). Names inferred expressions passed. diff Logical. TRUE, columns types differ across datasets shown. Default: FALSE. focus named length-1 character vector, e.g. c(year = "int"). supplied, named column shown, datasets type match expected type. Returns empty tibble (0 rows) types match, can used tests.

title: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
text: tibble column column one column per input dataset showing abbreviated type string ("int", "dbl", "chr", "fct", "date", "dttm", ...).

title: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
code: df1 <- data.frame(x = 1L, y = "a") df2 <- data.frame(x = 1.0, y = "b")  view_types(df1, df2) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   column df1   df2   #>   <chr>  <chr> <chr> #> 1 x      int   dbl   #> 2 y      chr   chr   view_types(df1, df2, diff = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   column df1   df2   #>   <chr>  <chr> <chr> #> 1 x      int   dbl   view_types(df1, df2, focus = c(x = "int")) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   column df2   #>   <chr>  <chr> #> 1 x      dbl

what: Write data frames to a presentable Excel file — write_excel
title: Write data frames to a presentable Excel file — write_excel
text: Creates new .xlsx workbook formatting applied: header row bold frozen, NA values shown blank cells, numeric columns formatted thousand separator displayed decimals (#,##0) column contains least one value >= 1000. Underlying values preserved. display format changes.

title: Write data frames to a presentable Excel file — write_excel
code: write_excel(   data,   path,   overwrite = FALSE,   as_table = FALSE,   freeze_header = TRUE,   skip_fmt = NULL,   detect_years = TRUE )

title: Write data frames to a presentable Excel file — write_excel
text: data data frame named list data frames. single data frame defaults sheet name "Sheet1"; unnamed list elements get names "Sheet1", "Sheet2", etc. path Path output .xlsx file. overwrite FALSE (default), aborts path already exists. Set TRUE replace file. as_table TRUE, data inserted Excel ListObject (table filter arrows banded rows). Default FALSE. freeze_header TRUE (default), freezes first row. skip_fmt Character vector column names exclude #,##0 number format. detect_years TRUE (default), numeric columns every non-NA value whole number 1800 2200 excluded #,##0 number format.

title: Write data frames to a presentable Excel file — write_excel
text: path, invisibly.

title: Write data frames to a presentable Excel file — write_excel
text: Year-like columns excluded number format automatically: numeric column every non-NA value whole number 1800 2200 assumed hold years, 2020 displayed 2.020. Set detect_years = FALSE disable heuristic, use skip_fmt columns guess (e.g. numeric period codes like 202001). modern .xlsx format supported. old binary .xls format written. Use append_excel() add sheets existing file.

title: Write data frames to a presentable Excel file — write_excel
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # New file with one sheet write_excel(mtcars, "out.xlsx")  # New file with multiple sheets write_excel(list(Cars = mtcars, Iris = iris), "out.xlsx")  # Insert as Excel table write_excel(mtcars, "out.xlsx", as_table = TRUE)  # Exclude a column from the number format write_excel(df, "out.xlsx", skip_fmt = "periode") } # }
